overview: National Institute of Technology Karnataka (NIT Surathkal), established in 1960, is one of India's premier technical institutions and an Institute of National Importance. It is especially known for its strong performance in engineering education, research, and placements, particularly in circuital branches.
accreditation: Institute of National Importance; All B.Tech programs accredited by NBA; NIRF 2024 Overall Rank: 17.

coursesAndFees.tuitionFee: ₹62,500 per semester (General/OBC, income > ₹5 lakh); ₹20,833/sem (income ₹1–5 lakh); ₹0 tuition (income < ₹1 lakh / SC/ST/PwD). Other fees ~₹36,400/sem. Hostel & Mess: ~₹39,000–₹43,000/sem.
Tuition varies; GATE-qualified students receive ₹12,400/month stipend. Median package: ₹10 LPA (2025).

placements.companiesVisited: Google, Microsoft, Amazon, Oracle, Goldman Sachs, Qualcomm, Uber, GAIL, BPCL, BEL
placements.internationalOffers: Increasing trend in high-value global offers in software and finance

keyTrends.highestPackageTrend: Highest package rose 15% from ₹55 LPA (2023–24) to ₹63.30 LPA (2024–25)

admissions.admissionProcess: B.Tech admissions through JoSAA counseling based on JEE Main rank; M.Tech admissions via GATE followed by CCMT counseling.
admissions.importantDates: Follows JoSAA and CCMT official schedules.

campus.specialNote: One-time fees (~₹13,000–₹25,000) included in first semester. Refundable caution deposit ~₹6,700. Sea-facing campus with strong hostel infrastructure.

contact.address: National Institute of Technology Karnataka, Surathkal – 575025, Karnataka, India
